steps.title: Find the contrast signal
steps.content: The key signal is “sharpened” paired with the contrast word “Although.” Sharpened means making something clearer and more focused. The sentence structure sets up: knowledge was once ____, but recent analyses have sharpened it. So the blank must describe an unclear or vague state.

steps.title: Match the word to the contrast
steps.content:
The blank needs to mean vague or unclear — the "Although" sets up a contrast where the old, uncertain state was then sharpened by recent analyses. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — Nebulous: knowledge was once "nebulous" — nebulous means hazy and unclear, perfectly contrasting with being sharpened into a clearer picture. ✓
  • Choice B — Intricate: knowledge was once "intricate" — intricate means complex and detailed, which is the opposite of what needs sharpening; you don't sharpen something already rich in detail. ✗
  • Choice C — Unprecedented: knowledge was once "unprecedented" — unprecedented means never seen before, which describes novelty rather than a lack of clarity. ✗
  • Choice D — Concrete: knowledge was once "concrete" — concrete means solid and definite, directly contradicting the need for recent analyses to sharpen it. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
